boards.name: ESP32 (Original / WROOM-32)
boards.best_for: General-purpose IoT, WiFi projects, entry-level. Good balance of power, connectivity, and price.

boards.name: ESP32-S3
boards.best_for: AI/ML at the edge (TensorFlow Lite), USB OTG, more GPIO. Native USB support. Better for battery-powered projects.

boards.name: ESP32-C3
boards.best_for: Cost-sensitive projects, RISC-V enthusiasts. WiFi + BLE 5.0 at lowest price. No analog pins.

boards.name: ESP32-C6
boards.best_for: WiFi 6 projects, Thread/Zigbee (with external radio). Best for future-proof IoT. Low power sleep modes.

boards.name: ESP32-C6-WROOM-1
boards.best_for: Space-constrained designs requiring WiFi 6. Compact form factor.

boards.name: ESP32-H2
boards.best_for: Matter smart home devices, Thread/Zigbee mesh networks. No WiFi but excellent for local mesh.

boards.name: Arduino Uno (ATmega328P)
boards.best_for: Education, simple projects, massive community support. Not for WiFi/BLE projects.

boards.name: Arduino Nano (ATmega328P)
boards.best_for: Breadboard projects, compact builds. Same as Uno but smaller form factor.

boards.name: Arduino Mega 2560
boards.best_for: Projects requiring many I/O pins, CNC/shield stacking. Largest Arduino I/O.

boards.name: Raspberry Pi Pico W
boards.best_for: Projects needing flexibility, PIO state machines, dual-core. Strong for audio, timing-critical tasks.

boards.name: Arduino Nano ESP32
boards.best_for: Arduino ecosystem + WiFi/BLE. Arduino Nano form factor with ESP32 capabilities.

boards.name: ESP8266 (NodeMCU)
boards.best_for: Ultra-budget WiFi projects. Limited GPIO but cheap. Legacy choice - ESP32-C3 is better for new designs.
